steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The phrase “ensuring that proteins retain their original configurations” completes the first clause. What follows—”TMAO is found in high concentrations...”—begins a new independent clause with its own subject and verb.

steps.title: Apply the sentence boundary rule
steps.content:
A period separates two independent clauses into separate sentences. Choice A is correct—it uses a period after “configurations,” properly creating two sentences.

The other choices violate this rule:
  • B fuses the clauses together with no punctuation
  • C creates a comma splice
  • D improperly uses “and” as a coordinating conjunction without proper punctuation


That matches choice A — A is correct.
